summary: Implemented and verified the P0-05 native instruction authority gate: scanned Codex/Claude/bootstrap instruction surfaces, detected native memory drift and unsafe authority conflicts, generated a durable status artifact, wired it into health/freshness/goal verification and Observatory state, and pushed app/data evidence. The broader OS completion objective remains partial because live pre-response proof, task lifecycle settlement, source/chunk/claim provenance, behavior recall/writeback, semantic retrieval/eval, Observatory browser proof, and install equivalence still remain.
decisions: Native instruction authority is treated as a P0 gate because restored GitHub/new-PC state must not be overridden by stale or conflicting local Codex/Claude instructions.
The scanner stores path, hash, timestamps, rule/evidence/finding metadata, but not raw native instruction text, to avoid durable leakage.
Hook approval and trust surfaces are allowed only as evidence that trust is visible/manual; instructions that bypass or auto-approve trust are blockers.
next_steps: Implement P0-06 source/chunk/claim provenance gate and make unsupported claims visible in health/goal verification.
Resolve remaining verify:goal blockers: live Codex Desktop pre-response proof, live context visibility, task lifecycle finish gate, and lifecycle auto-settlement.
Implement P1-09 behavior recall audit ledger and feedback writeback, then wire Observatory to show used memories, node usage, blocked/pending state, and audit trust results.
